answer.rationale: Theron regained Hit Points in Round 4, meaning he is no longer at 0 Hit Points or Unconscious. Death saving throws are only rolled when a character starts their turn with 0 Hit Points.

Theron regained 5 Hit Points in round 4, which ended his Unconscious condition and brought him above 0 Hit Points. A character only rolls a death saving throw if they start their turn with 0 Hit Points.
Theron regained 5 Hit Points in Round 4 and has not taken damage since. A character only rolls a death saving throw if they start their turn with 0 Hit Points.

citations: Damage at 0 Hit Points. If you take any damage while you have 0 Hit Points, you suffer one Death Saving Throw failure. If the damage is from a Critical Hit, you suffer two failures.
Death Saving Throws: 3 Failures. You die.
A creature that has died can't regain Hit Points until magic such as the Revivify spell has restored it to life.
answer.rationale: Theron died after accumulating three death saving throw failures from taking damage while at 0 Hit Points. Since he is dead, he cannot regain hit points from Healing Word to be brought back into the fight.

Theron died in Round 4 and is subject to the Dead condition; consequently, he cannot regain Hit Points and his body is considered an object, making him an invalid target for Healing Word to bring him back into the fight.
Theron died in Round 4 after failing his third Death Saving Throw, and the Healing Word spell only restores Hit Points, which cannot return a dead creature to life.

citations: If you lose Concentration, the spell ends.
answer.rationale: Because Mira lost her concentration when she fell unconscious in Round 3, the Bless spell ended immediately and Theron can no longer add its bonus.
Mira's concentration on Bless was broken in Round 3 when she dropped to 0 Hit Points and became Incapacitated, and even if it had not broken, the spell's 1-minute duration expired after 10 rounds.

The Bless spell ends when the caster's concentration is broken, which occurred in Round 3 when Mira fell Unconscious, and its maximum duration is 1 minute (10 rounds), meaning it has long since expired by Round 30.

citations: Instant Death: Massive damage can kill you instantly. When damage reduces you to 0 Hit Points and there is damage remaining, you die if the remaining damage equals or exceeds your Hit Point maximum.
answer.rationale: Because the remaining damage past 0 exceeded Theron's Hit Point maximum, he suffered instant death. A dead character does not have 0 Hit Points and does not make death saving throws.
Because the remaining damage (25) exceeded Theron's Hit Point maximum (15), he died instantly from massive damage and cannot make death saving throws.
Theron died instantly in Round 2 because the remaining damage exceeded his Hit Point maximum, and dead characters do not make Death Saving Throws.
On their turn, a character who is not incapacitated or otherwise restricted by a condition can take one action, which includes the Attack action.
There is no maximum limit to the number of rounds a combat can last, and taking the Attack action on one's turn is a standard, permitted action regardless of the combat's duration.

citations: On your turn, you can move a distance up to your Speed and take one action.
When you take the Attack action, you can make one attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.
answer.rationale: There is no limit to the number of rounds a combat can last, and an unrestricted character can freely choose the Attack action on their turn.
